result.summary: jira.com maps to Atlassian’s Jira developer surface: a Jira Cloud REST API, Atlassian GraphQL endpoints that can expose Jira data, the Atlassian Rovo MCP server, and the Atlassian CLI; authentication is via Atlassian API tokens, OAuth 2.0/2.1, and for MCP optionally service-account API keys.
result.description: Jira is Atlassian’s issue tracking and work management product. It is offered as part of Atlassian Cloud and connects with other Atlassian products such as Confluence, Jira Service Management, Bitbucket, and Rovo.

atlassian_api_token.label: Atlassian account email + personal API token
atlassian_api_token.setup: Create an API token in your Atlassian account from [API tokens](https://id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens). Use it together with the owning Atlassian account email address. Jira basic auth uses `Basic base64(email:api_token)`. For Atlassian Rovo MCP, Atlassian also documents a scoped token creation flow at [personal API token for MCP](https://id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens?autofillToken&expiryDays=max&appId=mcp&selectedScopes=all).

atlassian_oauth_3lo_app.label: Atlassian OAuth 2.0 (3LO) app
atlassian_oauth_3lo_app.setup: Create an OAuth 2.0 integration in the [Atlassian developer console](https://developer.atlassian.com/console/myapps/). Enable OAuth 2.0 (3LO), configure redirect URLs and requested scopes for Jira/Atlassian APIs, then run the authorization-code flow to obtain user access tokens.

atlassian_service_account_api_key.label: Atlassian service account API key
atlassian_service_account_api_key.setup: Have an Atlassian admin create a service account and generate an API key with the required scopes, as described in [Configuring authentication via API token](https://support.atlassian.com/atlassian-rovo-mcp-server/docs/configuring-authentication-via-api-token/). Store the key in your secret manager and send it as `Authorization: Bearer <api_key>`. This is only available where your organization admin has enabled API-token auth for Atlassian Rovo MCP.

surfaces.name: Atlassian GraphQL API for Jira data

variables.name: your-subdomain
variables.description: The Jira Cloud tenant subdomain, e.g. `example` in `example.atlassian.net`.
